issues: boss-battle context adds intensity that slightly undercuts purely cute/sunny target
reasoning: The explosion sprite itself uses warm orange/yellow/cream pixel-art burst with white sparkle pixels radiating outward in a round, bubbly shape — colors align well with the warm sunny palette (yellows, oranges, white light). The round pixel silhouette and sparkle fringe read as whimsical/magical rather than grimdark or aggressive. No out-of-tone palette shifts (no desaturated greys, no blood-red, no cold-corporate blues). However, the overall boss-battle scene context (grey stone dungeon walls, shadowy creature) already sits on the dungeon-crawler edge of the spectrum, and an 'explosion' VFX, even a cute one, carries inherent combat intensity. The VFX itself passes tone; the surrounding scene context pulls the composite marginally below a clean pass.
